understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with a '+'. If there are multiple '+' goal tiles, all of them must be occupied by selectable pieces at the same time for the level (and ultimately the full run) to report WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each action consumed 1 step from a limited step budget. A piece could be selected/controlled (the log always referred to a single 'selected piece'). Using CLICK on a grid position attempted to move the selected piece to the clicked tile; when the route/placement was legal the piece relocated (e.g., [9,21] -> [18,21]), and when it was not legal (due to walls/obstacles/blocked path or an invalid destination) the piece did not move. Walls/obstacles prevented movement and caused CLICK attempts to have no effect. Movement appeared to be constrained by blocking geometry (i.e., you cannot move through blocked tiles), and only successful moves changed the piece position while still consuming a step either way.

orient_history: Hypothesis: CLICK switches which selectable is active; movement applies to the currently selected piece, so we may need to select the other piece to make progress if this one is trapped.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves the currently selected selectable directly to the clicked empty cell (ignoring walls). To win, both selectables must end up occupying goal (+) tiles simultaneously; the goal region spans multiple cells so we can place both without overlap.

CLICK likely attempts to move/teleport the currently selected 3x3 piece to the clicked pixel location if that placement is valid; the previous CLICK [33,15] probably failed because it was outside the goal object's actual pixel bounds (goal starts at x=35,y=17). Movement keys are currently useless due to enclosure.

CLICK appears to select a piece when clicking on its position; movement is via MOVE_* and is blocked by collidable walls. Hypothesis: the other selectable may have a movable neighborhood even if the current one is boxed in.

Clicks appear to interact with grid pockets: clicking on the other selectable may swap/transfer the selected piece between pockets; clicks into the goal pocket failed, suggesting movement by click is constrained (e.g., only to valid pockets/occupied selectable pockets). Next I need to confirm whether clicking the other piece swaps or changes selection.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches selection (the engine reports the selected 'player' as moving to that selectable’s position). Movement of a piece is done via MOVE_* actions, but the currently selected piece is boxed in by walls. To find a movable piece/path, I should select the other selectable and re-check its movement options.

Hypothesis: MOVE actions are impossible due to enclosure by walls; progress likely requires CLICK interactions that act only when clicking the exact top-left position of an interactive object (e.g., selectable or goal). Clicking [18,21] worked because it matched a selectable’s exact position; clicking within the goal area but not at its exact position may not register.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable (switches active piece). MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) if not blocked by walls. To reach the goal, we likely must switch to the other selectable and see if it has any available movement or access to a corridor toward the goal.

CLICK appears to change which selectable is selected (not teleport), while MOVE_* attempts to move the selected piece by 1 cell (3 px) but can be blocked by walls. Need to see if the other selectable has any unblocked adjacent move.

Hypothesis: CLICK only has an effect when targeting a valid selectable node (teleporting the active piece to that node). Goal-clicks may require being at a specific node first (e.g., [18,21]) or may only work once positioned appropriately. Arrow moves are irrelevant here due to walls.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects/moves depending on where inside a tile you click. Clicking exactly on a cell corner may not register as clicking the goal tile; clicking inside the goal tile might teleport/move the selected piece onto that goal (despite walls), which is required to win since normal movement is impossible.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable changes selection; movement is grid-step but blocked by walls; there may be an edit/toggle mechanic where CLICKing a wall cell removes it (or otherwise changes passability), enabling navigation toward the goal pocket.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable toggles selection/teleports control between the two selectables even through walls; direct clicks on goals may only work after selecting the correct piece (or in a specific selection mode). Next I need to confirm selection can switch to the other piece from the current state.

CLICK appears to only interact with selectables (switch/select between them), not teleport to arbitrary tiles/goals. To make progress, we likely need to select the other piece and see if it has any legal MOVE actions toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves/teleports the selected piece to the clicked grid cell only when the click is exactly grid-aligned (multiples of 3). Failed CLICKs likely missed alignment. Therefore to reach the goal, click the goal cell's aligned top-left pixel coordinate (e.g., [33,15] for semantic cell [11,5]).

Hypothesis: movement-by-arrows is impossible here; progress likely requires CLICK-based teleport/placement. The goal area is the 3x3 '+' region (pixels roughly x=33..41, y=15..23). Previous clicks may have missed the effective target point inside the goal region, so try clicking nearer the center of the goal tile.

CLICKing on the other selectable likely swaps which piece is selected (previous logs show 'moved' between [9,21] and [18,21], consistent with selection switching). If one piece can move, we can route it toward the goal; otherwise we must discover another interaction trigger.

Hypothesis: CLICK must be on an exact interactive object anchor (e.g., the goal’s defined position) to trigger a move/teleport; near-miss clicks won’t work. Since movement is fully walled-in, winning likely requires teleporting onto the goal via a precise CLICK on the goal object.

CLICK appears to only switch which selectable is controlled (player position jumps to the clicked selectable). Non-selectable clicks (including on the goal) do nothing. Movement to the goal likely requires selecting the other piece and checking if it has an unblocked path via MOVE actions.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to reposition the selected 3x3 piece to the clicked grid-aligned top-left coordinate if that destination cell is valid (not wall/blocked). Prior failed clicks near the goal may have been misaligned to the underlying 3px cell grid; using a grid-aligned coordinate inside the goal area (multiples of 3) may succeed.

CLICK likely changes the selected piece when clicking on another selectable; the 'player position' then updates to that piece’s position. Since the current piece is immobile, we should switch selection to the other piece and check whether it has any available movement toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on the other selectable’s position relocates the currently selected piece to that pocket (possibly switching which pocket we control). Once in the other pocket, MOVE directions might open up toward the goal region.

Clicks seem to move the selected piece only when the clicked position shares the same row/column (teleport/slide rule ignoring walls). Prior successful clicks ([9,21] <-> [18,21]) were on the same y=21 row; failed goal clicks were not aligned. Hypothesis: click within the goal area on the same y=21 row should move onto the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches which piece is active; CLICK elsewhere may attempt a move/teleport but seems to fail unless some condition is met (possibly depends on which piece is selected). Since the current piece is immobile, try the other selectable as the active piece and then re-test interactions with the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves the currently selected selectable by one cell (3 px) if not blocked; CLICK on a selectable switches selection (the log reports this as the selected piece 'moved' to that selectable's position). To make progress we likely need to control the other selectable and see if it has an opening toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move the currently selected piece to the clicked pixel if that pixel is a valid grid-aligned landing spot (multiples of 3) and not blocked by rules; goal pixels that aren’t aligned may fail. Walls block MOVE_* entirely here, so solving likely requires finding the exact clickable landing coordinate inside the goal area (e.g., [39,21] or [39,18]).

CLICK likely targets an interior point of a goal cell/region to teleport/place the selected 1-cell (3x3px) piece; previous goal clicks may have failed due to hitting a boundary/non-interactive pixel. MOVE actions cannot work from the current pocket.

CLICK on a selectable likely switches control (or swaps pieces) between the two fixed pockets; arrow-move blockage may differ per pocket, so we should select the other piece and test if it has any unblocked movement toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches control/selection to that piece (the log reports it as the 'selected piece moved', but it is likely just changing which selectable is the active player). Direct MOVE may be possible for one selectable even if the other is trapped. To make progress with limited steps, we should switch to the other selectable and check its movement freedom.

CLICK appears to allow the selected piece to relocate directly onto certain clicked coordinates (notably onto the other selectable’s position), but clicks into/through wall-separated areas (e.g., near the goal) fail as 'blocked'. With only 2 steps left, best chance is to reposition to the other selectable then attempt a direct CLICK onto the goal.

Movement via MOVE_* is impossible here due to surrounding walls; progress must come from CLICK-based repositioning. CLICK likely moves the selected piece to the clicked valid target (possibly including the goal area). With only one goal object, we likely just need a selectable to occupy/overlap that goal area to trigger WIN.
Moves/CLICKs consume steps; with 0 steps remaining no further state changes are possible, so the level cannot be completed from this state.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with a '+'. If there are multiple '+' goal tiles, all of them must be occupied by selectable pieces at the same time for the level (and ultimately the full run) to report WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each action consumed 1 step from a limited step budget. A piece could be selected/controlled (the log always referred to a single 'selected piece'). Using CLICK on a grid position attempted to move the selected piece to the clicked tile; when the route/placement was legal the piece relocated (e.g., [9,21] -> [18,21]), and when it was not legal (due to walls/obstacles/blocked path or an invalid destination) the piece did not move. Walls/obstacles prevented movement and caused CLICK attempts to have no effect. Movement appeared to be constrained by blocking geometry (i.e., you cannot move through blocked tiles), and only successful moves changed the piece position while still consuming a step either way.
